frozen_components: LLM backbones (GPT-3.5, GPT-4, GPT-4o, o1-preview, Claude-3-Opus, LLaMA-2, Mixtral)
architecture.summary: EVAL is a verification and ranking framework for LLM-generated medical responses. Information flows from LLM outputs through similarity-based ranking (using Fine-Tuned ColBERT and other metrics) and a trained reward model; top-ranked responses are selected via rejection sampling. The system pairs learned similarity scoring with a supervised reward model trained on human-graded responses to filter and rank candidate answers.

biblio.title: Expert of Experts Verification and Alignment (EVAL) Framework for Large Language Models Safety in Gastroenterology

claims.statement: Fine-Tuned ColBERT achieved the highest alignment with human performance across three separate datasets
The reward model replicated human grading with 87.9% of cases across temperature settings

claims.dataset: evaluation set for UGIB (specifics not named)
claims.statement: significantly improved accuracy through rejection sampling by 8.36% overall
EVAL uses similarity-based ranking and a reward model trained on human-graded responses for rejection sampling

Evaluated OpenAI's GPT-3.5/4/4o/o1-preview, Anthropic's Claude-3-Opus, Meta's LLaMA-2 (7B/13B/70B), and Mistral AI's Mixtral (7B) across 27 configurations, including zero-shot baseline, retrieval-augmented generation, and supervised fine-tuning
